discrepancies.title: Post-flagship-cutoff operator-bandwidth collision risk surfaced
discrepancies.description: Synth AGGREGATE flagged biggest queue-level risk: Move 2 (N4) + Move 3 (N5) + Frontier-A Phase 1 verdict (Z7) all potentially wanting operator attention in 4-8 week post-flagship-cutoff window (~2026-07-15 to ~2026-09-15). Scope card v1.1 locked the cutoff trigger but did not solve the post-cutoff scheduling problem. This is a LOAD-BEARING new risk requiring explicit calendaring discipline before the cutoff fires.
discrepancies.discovered_by: synth verdict AGGREGATE clause (verbatim: 'Plan the post-cutoff calendar before the cutoff fires')
discrepancies.resolution_plan: Triage matrix v1.2 §G adds operator-bandwidth-collision schedule; CLI to draft post-cutoff calendar at next operator-action cycle (recommend before 2026-06-15 = scope card cutoff backstop pre-window)

discrepancies.title: Cite-budget interaction with Z7 conditional deposit forces forward planning
discrepancies.description: Synth Q-202-7 RISK_FLAG: ≤4 self-Zenodo cite limit (scope card v1.1 §VI lock) interacts with Z7 (Frontier-A P1 deposit). If flagship cites Z2 (SM) + Picture-chain + Umbrella + one other, Z7 takes the cite slot ONLY if load-bearing for Section 4. Cite budget must be planned NOW, not at flagship-submit-time.
discrepancies.resolution_plan: Verdict.md §5.3 pre-plans the cite budget: slots 1-3 = PCF-1 v1.3 / PCF-2 v1.4 / D2-NOTE v2.1; slot 4 = Z2 (SM consolidated deposit); Z7 substitutes slot 3 IF P1 closure is at d=3 (cubic extension territory), else operator-decision-required on ≤4 limit revision vs flagship-text reduction.
discrepancies.owner: CLI agent (pre-plan); operator (final cite-list call at submit-time)

discrepancies.title: PROMPT 202 cycle time ~6 minutes — verifying as deliberate operator workflow vs. system-anomaly
discrepancies.description: PROMPT 202 fired ~08:00 JST; verdict received ~08:06 JST. Cycle time ~6 minutes is fastest T1-Synth response in recent cycle history (compare slot 200 W3 ~24h cycle, slot 199B W2 ~few hours, typical T1-Synth dispatch ~1-24h). Possible explanations: (a) operator was actively at terminal and pasted to active claude.ai session; (b) prompt was structurally simpler than expected (well-scoped questions); (c) anomaly. No follow-up required; logged for cycle-time-pattern tracking only.
discrepancies.resolution_plan: No action; pattern-track in cycle-time discipline
